name: PsExec – PSEXESVC Service Key
categoryDescription: Registry artifacts for commonly abused tools with specific registry footprints: PsExec (temporary service key on target), FileZilla (FTP exfiltration), MEGASync (cloud exfiltration with autorun), Advanced IP Scanner (writes even as portable; operational history including scanned IP ranges), and NetSupport Manager (RMM abused as RAT). Includes artifact tips and companion file artifacts.
description: [PsExec] PsExec (Sysinternals) writes a temporary service key on the TARGET machine when executed remotely. The service is created to host the PSEXESVC.exe binary, which is dropped to C:\Windows and deleted after the session ends. The registry key itself may persist briefly after removal.
forensic_value: Presence of PSEXESVC service key is a strong IOC for lateral movement via PsExec. ImagePath points to C:\Windows\PSEXESVC.exe. Even after cleanup, LastWrite timestamp on the Services key parent may evidence the event. Correlate with Security Event 7045 (service installed) and 4624/4648 logon events.
